Football.title: KSI makes groundbreaking National League move just weeks after takeover deal
Football.content:
Dagenham & Redbridge are set to make history this weekend as they live-stream their National League match on YouTube.

Fans will be able to watch the clash with Hampton & Richmond on Saturday, March 28, for free via their co-owner KSI's channel.

2 KSI live-streamed his reactions from the stands at a previous game, but now he's going to stream full match coverage Credit: Getty

By doing so, they will become the first-ever National League club to stream a fixture on the platform in this fashion.



KSI is a global superstar and one of the most famous faces on YouTube, with his channel boasting over 17million subscribers.

He live-streamed his reaction from the stands when attending his first match as co-owner of Dagenham & Redbridge.

The viral sensation arrived at the club as part of their ownership group earlier this month, vowing to bring back the glory days.

But now, he's going to be showing full coverage of the action.

In a club statement, the Daggers wrote: "This groundbreaking collaboration marks the first time a National League match will be broadcast live on YouTube in this way, opening the doors of non-league football to a truly global audience.



"With thousands of new fans expected to tune in, the Club, partners at DAZN – the world’s leading sports entertainment platform – and the National League, are united in their ambition to make this the most-watched National League match of all time and set a new standard for the league.

"Beyond the occasion itself, the stream will shine a spotlight on the passion, quality, and authenticity of non-league football while introducing new audiences to the matchday experience at Dagenham & Redbridge."



KSI has also commented on the huge news, posting about it on his social media after the statement was released.

He wrote on X: "This Saturday is gonna be a movie! See you there."

Phil Anderson, CEO of the National League, said: “This collaboration highlights the power of embracing innovation in modern football.



"By bringing together clubs, creators, and a global streaming platform like DAZN, we’re opening new doors for visibility and engagement.

“Streaming this game in this way is a landmark moment not only for the National League but also for the wider Non-League game.

"Partnering with DAZN and tapping into KSI’s global audience gives us a unique chance to showcase what makes this level of football so special.

"Making the match free and available worldwide reiterates a shared ambition of the National League and DAZN to grow the game and reach new supporters.”

Dagenham & Redbridge have stated that further details of the stream will be shared closer to the game this weekend.



2 KSI has big plans as co-owner and the live stream is the first historic step Credit: Getty

KSI's Daggers plan

KSI was confirmed to have become a co-owner of the National League South side on March 3.

And he instantly set very high goals for his tenure in charge.

“Years ago, I saw the excitement, the happiness, the joy," the 32-year-old said. "I want to bring that back and then surpass it.

“I want the place to be pumping, I want it to be rocking, I want it to be an event whenever you come here."

KSI added: “With this club, I want to do the unthinkable. I want to take Dagenham and Redbridge to the Premier League.



“It’s obviously going to take time, but I’d say the realistic goal is getting out of this league and entering the National League."

Football.title: Cristiano Ronaldo left out as Mohamed Salah named in top ten greatest Premier League players ever
Football.content:
Does Cristiano Ronaldo have a place among the top ten greatest players to have ever graced the Premier League?

Former top-flight striker and talkSPORT host Gabby Agbonlahor doesn't think so...

5 Ronaldo has been snubbed by Agbonlahor in his top ten greatest players list Credit: Getty

5 Agbonlahor put Salah in his top four Premier League players of all time Credit: talkSPORT

After the news broke of Mohamed Salah leaving Liverpool at the end of the season, Agbonlahor put the Egyptian in his list.

But in somewhat of a shock, he snubbed Ronaldo, who scored over 100 league goals and won three titles for Manchester United.

Agbonlahor made the claim after being told of Jamie Carragher's recent comments on Salah's legacy in England.

Carragher wrote that the Reds legend will leave the Premier League ahead of Ronaldo in the 'pantheon of greats'.

And when told what Carragher said, Agbonlahor backed him.

The Breakfast co-host simply agreed: "Oh, definitely."

He then added: "Obviously not Ronaldo over his whole career, but definitely in the Premier League, I agree.

"Ronaldo wouldn't get into my Premier League greats."

Gabby's top ten Premier League players ever

Agbonlahor would later go on to select his top ten players that he has ever seen in the Premier League over the years.

And as he said, Ronaldo didn't make it, with another exclusive group of superstars having the privilege of being selected.

5 Salah, who is leaving Liverpool this summer, has been put among the greats Credit: AFP

Before announcing his top ten, the ex-Aston Villa forward clarified: "This is not about Premier League titles, this is not about goals scored or xG or how many passes were played forward, this is my top ten from watching players and how I enjoyed watching them."

Agbonlahor then revealed, in no particular order: "Thierry Henry, Alan Shearer, Wayne Rooney, Mohamed Salah, Steven Gerrard, Patrick Vieira, Kevin De Bruyne, Sergio Aguero, Frank Lampard...

"And, he didn't win anything at his club, but when he was at his best that season for Spurs, Gareth Bale, he was unbelievable."

After declaring his selections, co-host Ally McCoist jumped in to say: "Nobody will have any argument with seven of your ten.

"You might have one or two that are your own favourites that you enjoy watching, but Henry, Shearer, absolutely different level.

"Rooney, Salah, Gerrard and Lampard are absolutely in the equation.

5 Agbonlahor has placed Henry at the top of his list of Premier League icons Credit: Getty

Gabby's Premier League Top 10 Thierry Henry

Alan Shearer

Wayne Rooney

Mohamed Salah

Steven Gerrard

Patrick Vieira

Kevin De Bruyne

Frank Lampard

Sergio Aguero

Gareth Bale

5 Bale has made the list, with Giggs and Kane missing out Credit: Getty

"But the two I'd look at it is I don't see Harry Kane, I don't see Ryan Giggs.. .you don't have Roy Keane in there."

Agbonlahor replied: "Kane was unbelievable, but playing against Bale that season, every touch to the side, the way he ran past players.

"I'm on about that sort of play being hard to forget.

"I think I'd have to go Henry first, just behind him Shearer, then Rooney third and Salah in fourth, I'm putting him ahead of Giggs.

"Giggs has scored 109 goals in 632 games, Salah 191 in 323 games...bit of recency bias as well, but I'm going attacking-wise there.

"I'm going off attackers I loved to watch, as I was one myself."

Football.title: Jose Mourinho tipped for Premier League job this summer as pressure mounts on under-fire boss
Football.content:
Jose Mourinho tipped for Premier League job this summer as pressure mounts on under-fire boss

Newcastle have been urged to bring in Jose Mourinho to take the club to the next level.

Current boss Eddie Howe is coming under increasing pressure from a frustrated fan base amid an underwhelming season.

2 The Newcastle hierarchy are sticking with Howe for now Credit: Getty

The Magpies have failed to build on their Carabao Cup win in 2024/25, where they also finished fifth to qualify for the Champions League.

This season they are a lowly 12th, having dropped below rivals Sunderland after a 2-1 derby defeat at St James’ Park on Sunday.

A 7-2 thrashing at Barcelona has made it the week from hell for Newcastle fans with Howe in the firing line for fans’ ire.

talkSPORT understands the Newcastle hierarchy are continuing to back Howe, but face the prospect of no European football next season.

Eddie Howe replacements

talkSPORT’s Inside the Toon YouTube show discussed who could come in to replace Howe, who has been at Newcastle since November 2021.

Head of sport at The Sun, Shaun Custis, said: “I would take Mourinho.

“We talk about putting ourselves on a global level, about being in the conversation with all the big clubs around the world, and if you have a manager like Mourinho, he can attract players.

“He's also recognised around the world. It puts Newcastle on a different level.

“People now are interested in Benfica results. You weren't taking a blind bit of notice about Benfica results before.

“He has that charisma. He has won trophies. He is a winning manager. Box office boss.”

2 Mourinho is currently the manager of Benfica Credit: Getty

Former Chelsea, Man United and Tottenham boss Mourinho has strong connection to the north east club through his relationship with Sir Bobby Robson.

Robson mentored a young Mourinho at Sporting, Porto and Barcelona. Initially as his translator before transitioning into a coaching role.

talkSPORT.com’s Jack Cunningham added: “Mourinho's got that connection in Newcastle. The fans would get behind him.

“I just don't think he's at the level anymore that he was. I think that if you are getting a new manager, it has to be with a new squad anyway.

“At the moment, I think actually with this squad, how is he going to get the most out of it? I don't think if you kept the squad the same, another manager would come in and get much more out of the squad.

“The squad is the issue more so for me. But in terms of a new manager, I think [Como manager Cesc] Fabregas would be an exciting appointment.

Inside The Toon on talkSPORT talkSPORT does Newcastle like nowhere else. Stay ahead of every tactical shift, manager sacking and transfer rumour by making talkSPORT's Inside The Toon show your weekly go to for all things Magpies related. Subscribe now to ensure you never miss a beat of the action one episode at a time, sponsored by BetMGM. Download the app or visit BetMGM.co.uk. 18+gambleaware.org

“Sebastian Hoeness from Stuttgart got the best out of [Nick] Woltemade and is extremely highly rated as well.”

Next up for Newcastle after the international break is a trip to Crystal Palace, who are just three points behind the Magpies.

Then Howe's former club Bournemouth are visiting St James' Park.

News.title: Nursery admits corporate manslaughter after 14-month-old boy suffocated to death when staff forced him to nap
News.content:
TOT HORROR Nursery admits corporate manslaughter after 14-month-old boy suffocated to death when staff forced him to nap

A TODDLER was suffocated to death by nursery staff while he was forced to take a nap.

The nursery has pled guilty to corporate manslaughter after the 14-month-old died of asphyxiation.





Noah Sibanda was being cared for at Fairytales Nursery in Dudley, West Midlands, when staff tried to get him to fall asleep by “physically restraining” him on December 9, 2022.

He was tightly wrapped in a sleeping bag, had a blanket placed over his head and was laid face down on a cushion while staff member Kimberley Cookson, 23, restrained him using her leg.

The horror was caught on CCTV, and after a “considerable duration”, it was noticed that Noah was not breathing, and emergency services were called.

Ambulance crews rushed to the private nursery following reports he had gone into cardiac arrest but the tragic tot was pronounced dead in hospital.

Today, Fairytales Day Nursery Limited admitted one count of corporate manslaughter and a Health and Safety at Work Act offence at Wolverhampton Crown Court.

Nursery practitioner Cookson previously pleaded guilty to gross negligence manslaughter at the same court.

Director and business owner Deborah Latewood, 55, also admitted a Health and Safety at Work Act offence on the basis she did not know children were being put down to sleep in this dangerous way.

Ofsted previously closed the nursery in April 2023.



Alex Johnson, senior specialist prosecutor within the Crown Prosecution Service’s special crime division, said: “This case has been deeply distressing and represents every parent’s worst nightmare whenever they leave their young child at a nursery.

“Noah Sibanda should have been safe in the care of professionals entrusted with his wellbeing.

“He lost his life as a result of reckless and dangerous sleeping practices which posed an obvious and serious risk of harm.

“The evidence in this case, including CCTV footage and expert medical findings, showed that Noah was placed to sleep in a way that severely restricted his ability to breathe and move.



“The prosecution case was that these practices created a suffocating environment, from which a 14-month-old child, was clearly unable to escape.

“Fairytales Day Nursery Limited has now accepted criminal responsibility for the systemic failures that led to this tragedy, and Deborah Latewood has also acknowledged that the failings occurred under her management and oversight.

“Kimberley Cookson has also taken responsibility for her harmful actions.

“Nursery providers have a fundamental duty to protect the children in their care.

“This case underscores the devastating consequences of what happens when that duty is breached.



“Our thoughts remain with Noah’s family, who have endured an unimaginable loss.”

The defendants will be sentenced at Wolverhampton Crown Court on 16 April.

In a desperately sad tribute, Noah’s parents said in a statement: “Noah was a beautiful, happy and easy-going little boy and we miss him very much.”

Brave Masi, 31, said she and husband Thulani, 32, “would have been a really content family” of four.

She described Noah as a “handsome, beautiful, cute boy” who was very “lovable” and always smiling.



“I know parents are biased, but he was so beautiful,” she said.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

“He was calm, very relaxed and quite chilled and unfazed by things. We would find him just chilling with his arm behind his head.

“He was very easy-going and he made it easy as a first-time parent.”

News.title: Hundreds of tourists evacuated and three injured as fire rips through 5-star hotel in Paris after starting in basement
News.content:
AT least 400 people have been evacuated from a five-star hotel in Paris after a massive blaze broke out in a basement kitchen.

Tourists were forced to flee their rooms as two members of staff tried to put out the fire which began in the basement kitchen of the famous Palace Le Bristol.





It’s understood three people are being treated for injuries.

Firefighters said the “fairly significant” fire began around 11.20am local time on Wednesday.

The hotel, featured in Woody Allen’s 2011 film Midnight in Paris, is often used by visiting world leaders and celebrities.

Around 100 firefighters are on scene, the Paris Fire Brigade confirmed.

Police have warned Parisians and tourists to avoid the area of rue du Faubourg Saint-Honoré until the blaze is contained.

The historic hotel celebrated its centenary last year, marking 100 years of luxury, elegance, and French art de vivre since its opening in 1925.

A hotel spokesperson told Le Parisien “everything is under control”.



“The safety of our guests and employees is our top priority,” they said.

The luxury hotel, not far from Élysée Palace, is owned by the German multinational Oetker.

More to follow… For the latest news on this story keep checking back at The Sun Online.

Thesun.co.uk is your go-to destination for the best celebrity news, real-life stories, jaw-dropping pictures and must-see video.

Like us on Facebook at www.facebook.com/thesun and follow us from our main Twitter account at @sunbizarre.

News.title: ‘You’re a publicist for a paedophile!’ Moment Ed Balls skewers Huw Edwards’ rep who thinks he shouldn’t pay back £200k
News.content:
THIS is the moment Huw Edwards’ rep is branded “a publicist for a paedophile” in a fiery TV interview.

Ed Balls hit out at the former newsreader’s publicist Barry Tomes on Good Morning Britain.





The rep appeared on the show following a Channel 5 drama which charts how Edwards, 64, groomed a young man on social media while paying him for explicit images.

The Sun exposed his shocking behaviour three years ago.

Ed and Susanna Reid interviewed the “self-appointed” publicist who said he wanted to find out “why this happened” and Edwards wanted to “tell his side”.

Fuming Ed lashed out and said: “The problem is, he is a paedophile and you are his publicist, which means that you are a publicist for a paedophile.



“I mean it was worse than that, and then he goes on to use his mental illness as a excuse. He goes on to say he hopes that will explain how he behaved.”

Tomes said that the main reaction to the Channel 5 drama was to praise Martin Clunes‘s portrayal of Edwards.

He said: “Shouldn’t those conversations be about the awful thing that happened?

“Shouldn’t we be talking about that side of it? I want to find out why this happened.



“He’s not the first famous man to have these issues and there’ll be more unless we can figure out why”.

He went on to agree that “mental illness is not an excuse for criminality” but said “maybe it’s an explanation as to why you do things that are out of character”.

Edwards has ignored BBC pleas to return the £200,000 he was paid after his arrest on paedophile charges.

The corporation’s chairman said that the former News at Ten anchor has repeatedly been asked to “just give it back”.

Edwards was on £475,000 a year when he was arrested in November 2023 over child abuse images.



He was not sacked, even though some senior BBC staff knew he had been held and was allowed to step down on medical grounds in April 2024.

Asked whether Edwards should return the £200,000 salary that followed his suspension, Tomes said: “I wouldn’t pay it back. He had a contract, his contract was terminated and he was paid.

“If they think they should have it back, have they started legal proceedings? I’m not sure, but I personally wouldn’t, no.”



Tomes said the Channel 5 documentary Power: The Downfall of Huw Edwards made Edwards feel like he needed to respond “earlier than he is ready”.

The ex-BBC presenter spoke ahead of the drama, for the first time since The Sun exposed his behaviour three years ago.

In a whining statement, Edwards said: “[Programme producers Wonderhood] made no attempt to check with me the truth of any aspect of their narrative before going ahead with the production.

“They belatedly asked for a response after the drama had been made, while reserving the right to edit any such response.

“They also refused to disclose whether any of those making allegations had been paid for their contributions.



“Channel 5’s ‘factual drama’ is hardly likely to convey the reality of what happened.”

The broadcaster separately pleaded guilty to three counts of making indecent images of children, and in September of the same year he was given a six-month prison sentence, suspended for two years.

He didn’t hand himself in but was caught after another paedophile was arrested who had been sending him hideous images.

During the police investigation, a number of WhatsApp messages was found on his phone including an exchange with the man who was later convicted of sex offences.

Paedophile Alex Williams sent Edwards 377 images via the app, including 41 indecent images of children, some showing a victim aged between seven and nine.



Edwards’ statement goes on to say: “My deep regret and remorse for the crimes I committed were expressed in court.

“In pleading guilty at the earliest opportunity, I took full responsibility for my reprehensible actions.

“I am repelled by the idea that some people enjoy viewing indecent images of children.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

“Every image represents an innocent victim. I offer my sincere and profound apologies for what I did.”

Edwards also said he plans to tell his side of the story, but added it is a “slow process given the fragile state of my health“.

News.title: Teen, 18, recorded himself strangling & beating mum to death with hammer before making sick online boasts about murder
News.content:
A TEENAGER recorded himself beating his own mother to death in a horrifying four-hour attack – before boasting about the murder online.

Tristan Roberts, 18, talked about killing his “terrified” mum Angela Shellis in online posts and recordings before he murdered her in a park in Wales.





He was today jailed for life with a minimum term of 22 years and six months.

Roberts spent hours every day on the gaming and chat platform Discord, Mold Crown Court heard.

He planned the killing for three weeks. His horror attack lasted from 11pm until 3.30am – and he recorded it the entire time as a “trophy”.

On the day of the murder, he said in a voice recording: “This is Tristan Roberts. Tonight I’m going to be Alex and I’m going to murder my mother with a sledgehammer.”

The teen changed his online status to: “Tonight’s the night.”

The prosecutor told the court the teen “was in some way role playing”.

In one of his online posts he wrote: “TBH with you I think I nearly traumatised myself today by putting my shoes on at like midnight when she was sleeping and having the hammer in my hand.”



Another read: “She’s going to vanish off the earth. I’ve tried everything else. Now it’s time.”

He later logged on and boasted about what he had done.

A four-hour voice recording detailed how Roberts first attacked his mum in her bedroom, inflicting bruising to her face and strangulation injuries.

It was so chilling that the judge decided not to play it in court.

The mum had been kept prisoner in her room for hours as her son brandished a hammer.



He convinced her to leave the house under the pretence that she would have her injuries tended to at a local pub – but instead she was killed.

The court heard after killing his mother, 45, with at least four heavy blows to the head, Roberts recorded himself saying: “Oh God that was terrifying…That felt so crazy.”

Roberts bought knives, axes and hammers after turning 18 – the legal age limit for these purchases – 10 days before the chilling attack in October last year.



In the weeks before the haunting murder, he’d posted disturbing content, openly talking about killing his mum – which he then recorded on a dictaphone.

AI platform DeepSeek suggested Roberts use a hammer rather than a knife to kill her, when he told it he was writing a book and asked for the best way to kill someone.

He also had a fascination with the US crime TV series Dexter and horror film American Psycho.

The court heard how he had a hatred for “girls” and for his mum in particular.

The mum-of-two was discovered with multiple blunt force injuries to the head in the seaside town of Prestatyn, North Wales, on October 24 last year.



There was a lengthy trail of blood along a nearby gravel path in the nature reserve.

A crutch, black gloves and black bloodied balaclava – which he forced his mum to wear – were discovered at the scene.

It later emerged Angela had needed a crutch because of a recent knee injury.

At first North Wales Police struggled to identify Angela after finding her at 8.30am – but at 1pm a worried relative phoned the force.

The family member was concerned after hearing reports that a woman’s body had been discovered.



Not long after, Roberts was arrested at the family home – where he lived with his mum – and charged with murder four days later.

When he was arrested, he was described as calm and compliant and he asked cops: “Is the body you found my mother?”



Cops found Angela’s phone hidden under a suitcase in a wardrobe when they raided the home.

Family had tried to phone her and received messages back – thought to be Tristan posing as his mum to quell their concerns.

He told his older brother – who was away at university – that their mum was asleep in bed.

Ring doorbell footage showed the mum and son leaving their home on foot at 3.19am.

CCTV footage showed the pair walking over a railway crossing, with Roberts carrying a rucksack containing the murder weapon.



He returned home alone at 5.35am, and later left the house carrying a carrier bag thought to have contained bleach to clean the murder scene.

The teen had autism and ADHD and a previous offence as a youngster for possessing a bladed article.

Police have submitted an application for a safeguarding review.

Sentencing him at Mold Crown Court, Judge Rhys Rowlands said it was a “truly awful way to die” and Angela “cared for and worried about you”.

“She would have been in pain and terrified. She could be heard saying that you were hurting her and to call 999 and get help for her and let her go.



“You ignored her pleas and managed to leave the house on the pretext you would walk to town to get help and got her to wear a balaclava.”

The mum’s heartbroken family said: “She had so many amazing qualities, but one of the greatest was the way she devoted herself to her boys.

“She was a fantastic and fiercely supportive mum, the kind who never gave up, no matter how hard life became.

“She fought tirelessly for them, and her love for them was unbreakable, a source of strength that carried her and her boys through every challenge.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

“Her kindness was genuine, her laughter infectious, and her presence brought warmth into every room. She had a way of making people feel seen, valued, and loved, and that is something rare and precious.



“Losing her has left a space that can never truly be filled. She will be missed every single day by so many people whose lives she touched.”

News.title: Met Police publish 999 call transcript with Morgan McSweeney over stolen phone
News.content:
MORGAN McSweeney did not tell the Met Police he worked for the Prime Minister when he called 999 to report his phone theft, a bombshell transcript reveals.

Scotland Yard made a highly unusual decision to release details of the call after they faced questions over why they did not pursue an investigation into the stolen phone.



There are fears that important messages about Lord Peter Mandelson’s appointment as British ambassador to the US could have been lost in the theft.



Cops stressed they did not know Mr McSweeney was employed by Sir Keir Starmer or the “particular security risks” associated with his device.

“This was not information provided to us and could not reasonably have shaped our decision making,” the Met said.

A transcript from the call, made on October 20 last year just before 10.30pm, shows Mr McSweeney did tell the cops the phone was a Government handset.

The Met revealed they made two attempts to phone him during working hours the next day – but there was “no answer”.



They said they reviewed available CCTV cameras in the area which did not identify any “realistic lines of enquiry” before closing the case.

MCSWEENEY'S CALL TO COPS An extract from the phone call Call handler: Can you remember anything about his appearance? Caller: Yeah, he was young. He was a black guy. He was on a bike. Call handler: About how young? Call handler: Just a guess. Caller: Teens. Late teens. Call handler: Was he skinny, tall, any idea? Caller: Yeah. He was slim. He was about average height. Call handler: Was it an e bike or pedal bike? Caller: Pedal bike. Call handler: Have you got a tracker on the phone at all? Caller: I do. It’s a government phone. Call handler: And it’s your work phone. What kind of phone is it? Caller: It’s an iPhone. Call handler: Do you know what model? Caller: I don’t.

However, during the emergency phone call to police, Mr McSweeney wrongly gave the location as Belgrave Street, in Tower Hamlets, instead of Belgrave Road in Westminster.

The Met has now amended the error and is reassessing whether there is evidence available.

Mr McSweeney dramatically quit as Sir Keir’s chief of staff last month amid fierce scrutiny over his role in Lord Mandelson’s appointment to US ambassador.



Furious Labour MPs blamed Mr McSweeney for pushing for Mandelson to be given the job – despite the peer’s friendship with paedophile financier Jeffrey Epstein being public knowledge.

The Labour Government has been forced by MPs to release documents surrounding Mandelson’s links to Epstein before he was handed the Washington job.

Tory leader Kemi Badenoch tore into the debacle during Prime Minister’s Questions.

In a dig at Sir Keir, she told the Commons: “I wonder what a director of public prosecutions would make of the defence, ‘sorry, I can’t produce my WhatsApps, my phone’s been stolen’?”

Earlier today Health Secretary Wes Streeting claimed Mr McSweeney’s phone theft was a “cock-up not conspiracy”.



The Cabinet Minister said the former aide could not have known his messages would be needed for a probe into Lord Mandelson’s appointment as US ambassador.

He told Times Radio: “I can totally understand the cynicism in these sorts of cases, but do bear in mind that when his phone was stolen, it was reported to the police at the time.

“He couldn’t at that stage have known that Parliament would ask for the publication of all messages in the way that they have in quite an unprecedented way.

“Yes, sometimes in politics things can be explained as a result of conspiracy. In other times, more often than not, it is a result of cock-up rather than conspiracy.”

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Concerns have also been raised over the fact the phone was not backed up.



Lord Mandelson was sacked from his Washington role in September last year over his links with Epstein.

News.title: Glam model, 50, who wove ‘web of lies’ to con adoring men into spending huge sums to fund her lavish life is jailed
News.content:
A GLAMOUROUS Vogue model who conned men into spending extensive sums of money to fund her lavish lifestyle has been jailed.

Romance fraudster Gemma Kingsley, 50, wove a “web of lies” to convince adoring men to fork out tens of thousands of pounds.





The “deceptive” mum-of-four tricked her victims into a relationship, while telling them she was due to inherit a large fortune.

Kingsley then used that to leverage them into spending huge sums on her behalf, including on luxurious cars and a planned wedding.

At other times, the “charming” blonde stole from her unsuspecting lovers or used false bank card details to pay thousands of pounds on hotel stays.

Appearing at Swindon Crown Court for sentencing today, the romance fraudster was jailed for seven years and seven months.

Her victims, some of whom have described her as a Walter Mitty character, include wealthy businessmen and an ex-serviceman.

Have you been affected by Kingsley’s crimes or know someone who has? Email samuel.creed@news.co.uk

One, who wanted to remain anonymous, told the Daily Mail: “This isn’t some hot blonde in heels who has bamboozled a couple of guys, not at all.



“This was a woman who was well-spoken, educated, not unattractive and who used complex means to deceive people and gain their trust.”

Another victim was left “mortified and humiliated” after their wedding at Cliveden House, in Berkshire, was cancelled at the last minute.

The lavish event was called off with weeks to go, despite his family in Australia forking out thousands of pounds.

The court heard Kingsley had a previous conviction in France for fraud by false representation.

The model had used one of her victim’s cards to spend almost 100,000 euros on 5-star hotel stays in the popular French ski resort Albertville.



She was handed a six-month suspended prison sentence, a 6,000 euro fine and was banned from staying in France for five years.

Kingsley, of Beadnell, Northumberland, had pleaded guilty during a hearing at the same court last month.

She admitted six counts of theft and four counts of fraud by false representation and two counts of using a false instrument with intent that it will be accepted as genuine.

Kingsley also pleaded guilty to one count of being in possession of an article for use in fraud.

The fraudster’s web of lies began to unravel when Wiltshire Police were initially tipped off to her crimes in 2019.



Officers received a report she had fraudulently used the bank details of her mother’s friend to pay for a stay at Cromhall Farm, near Chippenham.

Cops then launched a probe into Kingsley, prompting other forces to pass on information about ongoing investigations into the model.

She told one victim she was due to receive a multi-million-pound inheritance, even producing forged letters from professionals as proof.

He then went on to spend tens of thousands of pounds on furniture for a new home, dental bills, her debts and a payment for a wedding.

The victim forked out vast amounts of cash on the assumption he would get the money back through the inheritance.



Kingsley repeatedly used his bank cards to steal money and even opened a betting account in his name.

She went on to lose huge amounts gambling his money and the relationship eventually ended in 2018.



The following year, she met another victim, again misleading him into believing she was about to inherit a large fortune.

He helped her with significant deposits for a Land Rover and a Porsche, as well as expensive hotel stays.

The victim also racked up a large debt through her use of his bank cards, before entering into two brief relationships in Autumn the same year.

The 50-year-old repeatedly used the victims’ personal and company bank cards to make purchases without their knowledge.

Wiltshire Police’s fraud team found Kingsley had been committing theft and fraud by falsifying documents and letters.



Numerous other victims included solicitor’s firms, lawyers and a bank.

Kingsley was first interviewed about the offences in May 2020, where she denied all offences and bizarrely claimed to be the victim herself.

From January 2021, officers repeatedly attempted to interview her for a second time but were unable to make contact with her.

She was eventually tracked down to a property in Bristol in August 2022, where she was arrested for a second time – again denying the offences.

Last March, the Crown Prosecution Service authorised charges against Kingsley, who was living in a remote cottage in the Scottish Highlands.



Despite receiving summons, she failed to attend Swindon Magistrates’ Court in June, claiming not to know what she had been charged with.

A warrant was later issued for her arrest and, the same month, Kingsley was pulled over by Police Scotland after speeding on the Isle of Skye.

She was arrested and remanded into custody.

After several months of delaying court hearings, she eventually pleaded guilty to 13 offences against her.

DC Melissa Pope, from the Wiltshire Police fraud team, said Kingsley’s crimes caused “significant emotional anxiety” for her victims.



She added: “She manipulated their emotions, assuring them through her future inheritance she would be able to repay the considerable sums…

“I hope that her victims can now start to move on from this experience.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

“I want to highlight that for romance fraud cases like Kingsley’s, while there are significant financial losses, it is often the emotional impact on the victims that has a more lasting impact.

“It is a huge breach of trust and self-esteem which can take years to recover from.”

News.title: Moment carer who ‘murdered’ disabled man says ‘he’s been dead for 12 days’ as medics find body hidden under duvet in bed
News.content:
THIS is the moment a carer who allegedly murdered a disabled man says “he’s been dead for 12 days” as the body is found under a blanket in bed.

Leon Pratt’s decomposing body was found wrapped in a duvet at his home after concerned neighbours phoned police about a “fishy” smell.





The 64-year-old was being looked after by Jason Trundle, who told paramedics “he’s been dead for 12 days” as he let them in the house in Telford, Shropshire.

Trundle, 54, said he found the man dead and denied murdering him, saying: “And no, before you ask, I didn’t kill him” on October 26, 2024.

He told paramedics: “I’ve got nowhere else to go,” when they asked why he didn’t tell anyone about the dead body.

Trundle moved in with Leon after they met on social media and he said he was homeless.

Leon, who had several health conditions and used a wheelchair and walking sticks, invited the man to live with him.

The unofficial carer – who had known Leon for a year and a half – helped look after him on a daily basis.

But neighbours became worried after they hadn’t seen Leon for months and smelled “something putrid like rotting flesh” coming from the house.

A post-mortem examination found Pratt had internal injuries to his ribs, lower back, throat and neck.



Worcester Crown Court heard that in the week before, Trundle told neighbours the victim had threatened to hit his pet dog with his walking stick.

He claimed Leon had “gone a bit crazy” and “proper lost it” so he had to put him in a headlock.

“He was off the plot, going stupid, in the sense that every time I went out to do shopping or anything like that, I had to lock him in the house because he would literally spiral out of control,” he claimed.

Trundle has admitted manslaughter, but he is on trial for murder.

He pleaded guilty to preventing a lawful burial and fraud by false representation after using Pratt’s bank card in a shop while he lay dead.



Prosecutor Richard Barraclough KC told the court: “That means he accepts that he unlawfully caused the death of Mr Pratt by a deliberate act, we would say by assaulting him, knowing he would do him some harm.

“The Crown alleges that what he did to Mr Pratt was far more serious than simply manslaughter. The Crown alleges he murdered Mr Pratt.

“The Crown alleges that when the defendant caused the death of Mr Pratt, as he accepts he did, he must have intended to at least cause him really serious harm.”

The trial continues.

News.title: UK’s most ridiculous council fines from kids playing in street to SWEARING – all the busybody rules you need to know
News.content:
BUSYBODY councils have dished out on-the-spot fines for swearing in public, feeding birds and smoking in company cars.

The Sun can reveal how authorities doled out thousands of £100-a-go penalties for “petty” offences – including playing games in the street and failing to take in the bin.





Some 143 councils in England and Wales have hit people with more than 44,000 penalty notices under so-called Public Space Protection Orders in the past three years.

Data revealed under freedom of information laws showed town halls doling out fines for “wild bird feeding”, “obscene gestures” and “not presenting bins correctly”.

In posh Greenwich, south-east London, officials fined 185 people for swearing or making “obscene gestures”.

In north-east Lincolnshire, two people were fined for smoking in company vehicles – while another 12 were punished for using metal detectors.



Plymouth Council doled out 1,511 fines between 2023 and 2025 for “litter-related” offences, while Boston Council fined 37 people for feeding birds.

In Cambridge, two fines were issued to people trying to tout river punting trips.

Councils in Leeds, Sunderland and Newark punished homeowners for failing to “present bins correctly” or leaving them out.

Doncaster Council issued one fine to people playing “street games”.



More councils issued the on-the-spot penalty notices for dog offences than any other type of misdemeanour – with thousands of fines given for failing to pick up dog mess or even bringing along a poop-scoop on walks.

Fines were issued by councils for dogs being off lead on beaches, in parks and cemeteries.

In the London borough of Redbridge, 422 men were fined for attempting to “buy sex”, while hundreds of others across England were penalised after being caught spitting, urinating and even defecating in public.

Public Spaces Protection Orders (PSPOs), allow councils to ban activities in public spaces and can carry a maximum fine of £1,000 on prosecution.

Thousands of fines were handed out for unauthorised public drinking and vehicle nuisance.



The top issuing council was Hillingdon in north London with 9,093 – though it refused to document what the fixed penalties related to.

Hounslow gave out 6,006 fines – all for boozing in public – followed by fellow London borough Brent with 4,922, many for spitting, drinking alcohol and setting off fireworks.

The PSPOs were intended to clamp down on threatening or violent behaviour.

But campaigners say they have been used to punish petty behaviour, with fines bizarrely handed out for “stunts”, “smoking at work” and “overstaying a mooring”.

The Campaign for Freedom in Everyday Life said councils currently have more than 2,000 active PSPOs restricting activities including busking, begging, loitering, rough sleeping, charity collection, standing in groups, and ball games.



Director Josie Appleton said: “It’s very worrying that councils are continuing to slap people with fines for such everyday actions.

“These penalties are soon to be hiked to a massive £500 – given that most fines are issued by private companies on commission, this is a recipe for mass fleecing of the public.

“We urge the government to reform these powers, to ensure that people are only penalised when they have actually done something harmful.”

Tandridge District Council was last month revealed to be considering a ban on “excessive horn honking”.

In Bingley, West Yorkshire, a driver was fined £100 for honking his horn at female joggers and shouting: “Go on ladies, get running”.



Chief Inspector Nick Haigh, of Bradford Police, said: “We want to make it absolutely clear: beeping horns, shouting out of car windows and harassing women out running isn’t acceptable and will not be tolerated.”

The Sun revealed last month how a young woman was fined £150 by Manchester “litter police” for feeding a pigeon a scrap of her McDonald’s wrap.

Sam Ekins, 23, tore off a tiny piece of tortilla for the hungry bird in Manchester city centre, only for enforcement officers to swoop in and accuse her of littering.

She was fined under PSPO powers outsourced to a private third party company.

Sam, who works as a line chef for Wetherspoon‘s, said: “We got McDonald’s Wrap of the Day and sat a few feet from the entrance eating it.



“A pigeon came swooping up and I just thought I’d feed it. I didn’t think twice about pulling a tiny bit of my wrap off.

“The next thing I knew these two big security guards came out of nowhere saying they’re from Manchester City Council.

“They said I shouldn’t have discarded food and that I was liable for a £150 fine.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

“I couldn’t believe it. It was literally just pulling off bits of a tortilla. It’s not like I chucked a whole meal on the floor and legged it.”

A Manchester City Council spokesperson said: “The throwing of rubbish, including items of food, is classed as littering and where appropriate, enforcement officers will issue a Fixed Penalty Notice if an offence is observed.”

News.title: Senior Reform figure says he'd 'had a bit to drink' before homophobic George Michael joke
News.content:
Senior Reform figure says he'd 'had a bit to drink' before homophobic George Michael joke

Lord Malcolm Offord explained he'd been drinking when he made a cruel sexual gag about the star's grieving partner Fadi Fawaz at a rugby club dinner, which resulted in a furious backlash.

View 2 Images George Michael is a national treasure (Image: Getty Images)

Reform’s leader in Scotland has claimed he "had a bit to drink" the night he cracked a vile homophobic joke about the partner of pop legend George Michael.

‌



Lord Malcolm Offord had made a cruel sexual gag about the star's grieving partner Fadi Fawaz at a rugby club dinner, which resulted in a furious backlash.

‌



A witness told The Daily Record he was stunned by a speech peer Lord Offord - who was made a Tory peer in 2022 before defecting to Reform - gave during a Burns Night speech at the London Scottish rugby club. It was peppered with references to Michael, who died in 2016, witness Ian Lewer said.

‌



Senior Reform figure apologises after vile homophobic George Michael joke READ MORE :

View 2 Images Lord Offord explained 'he'd had a bit to drink' (Image: PA Wire)

Lord Offord has since apologised and made a donation to an LGBT rugby club. Taking to social media, Lord Offord admitted he had "had a bit to drink" on the night in question and that he regretted making members of the audience uncomfortable.

He said: "The media have been having another pop at me, so here are the full facts for the public to judge for themselves. Eight years ago, in 2018, long before I was appointed a government minister in the Lords, I gave the Burns Night after-dinner speech at a rugby club before a large crowd of members and their friends, wives, husbands, and partners.

‌



Ensure our latest headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as your Preferred Source in your Google search settings.

"I must admit, I’d had a bit to drink by then, and finished with a crude joke about the late George Michael, as I had been connecting his songs to Burns’s works. It was intended to be funny through being edgy and shocking, but I’m no Frankie Boyle or Ricky Gervais. Instead of being funny, I made members of the audience uncomfortable and instantly regretted it.

"I immediately apologised to the club, and made amends by voluntarily donating to the King’s Cross Steelers, the original gay-inclusive rugby union club, to support their travel and participation in the Bingham Cup in Amsterdam. I then also arranged a fixture between my club and theirs. So that’s the whole story of what happened eight years ago. Most of the media won't publish the joke, but I see that the general gist has now been reported, and I don’t want to sully your screens with something so crude."

‌



Mr Lewer, 41, told The Daily Record about the event in January 2018: “I was sitting next to a gay man and it was clearly an extremely uncomfortable and unpleasant experience for him. At the time I thought it pretty awful and indeed that was the feeling in the room. Even for a rugby club it was a crude, bad taste and insulting spectacle. I’m not a prude, I’m up for banter. But there is banter and taking things to that level. I don’t know who in their right mind would say something like that. It is utterly bizarre that anyone would get up and make a speech in a room with 200 people and say something like that.

"It was so shocking I’ve not forgotten it. I remembered it again when I became aware he had become leader of Reform in Scotland. He seems to have a strong chance of becoming an MSP, perhaps a powerful MSP. felt people in Scotland should know before they cast their votes.”

The incident has sparked widespread condemnation. Scottish Labour equalities spokesman Paul O’Kane said: “This just exposes Reform as the nasty party that they are. Whether it is homophobia or racism, there is clearly no low to which they will not sink."

Article continues below

First Minister Mr Swinney tweeted: “These comments have no place in Scottish society. They are typical of the prejudice spouted by Reform. Malcolm Offord and his ilk are unfit for office.”

Lord Offord defected to Reform last year and was hand picked by Nigel Farage to lead the party in Scotland.

News.title: Chinese astronaut heard mysterious 'knocking' sound from vacuum of space
News.content:
Chinese astronaut heard mysterious 'knocking' sound from vacuum of space

Taikonaut reported eerie knocking in the quietness of space - and others have heard it too

View Image Taikonaut Yang Liwei blasted off aboard Shenzhou 5 in 2003 (Image: STR, BTV/AFP via Getty Images)

China's inaugural astronaut returned to Earth with an extraordinary account after encountering a puzzling 'knocking sound' that left space engineers completely stumped.

‌



Taikonaut Yang Liwei launched into orbit aboard Shenzhou 5 in 2003 for a 21-hour mission that saw him circle the globe 14 times before touching down safely in Mongolia.

‌



However, during his journey through space, Yang detected something utterly inexplicable: a distinct 'knocking' sound repeating itself continuously. He examined the capsule thoroughly, gazed through the porthole, and inspected the panels, yet couldn't identify the source, even though the sound persisted.

‌



He maintained it wasn't originating from inside the capsule, but rather from outside, in the vacuum of space, where sound, theoretically, cannot travel.

Yang subsequently revealed to the BBC: "It sounded like someone hitting the hull, like striking an iron bucket with a wooden hammer."

He remained composed and continued his mission, ultimately dismissing the unsettling tapping as a 'normal phenomenon'.

‌



Odds of NASA astronauts returning from Moon, AI turning us all into a brainless army, the North Sea asteroid, and why you should be grateful to have an appendix - all this and more in our latest weird science newsletter

Upon returning to Earth, engineers meticulously examined the spacecraft for answers but discovered nothing that could explain the noise, reports the Daily Star.

Yet the peculiar sound didn't disappear with Yang, as Chinese media subsequently reported that crews on later missions - including voyages in 2005 and 2008 - also experienced the same mysterious knock. Prior to launch, Yang even warned newcomers not to be alarmed if space appeared to come knocking at the door.

‌



Sound requires a medium, such as air or metal, to travel, so a knock from the vacuum makes no sense.

Potential explanations put forward included tiny micro-debris striking the hull without leaving visible marks. While plausible, no evidence was discovered during Yang's mission.

Alternative theories included thermal fractures as the spacecraft experiences extreme temperature fluctuations every 90 minutes in orbit, and structural or mechanical vibrations triggered by pressure systems, valves, reaction wheels or attitude thrusters.

Article continues below

China's more recent spacecraft are equipped with additional sensors, and every unusual creak and tremor gets recorded. However, until the data provides a conclusive answer, Yang's 'knock-knock' remains amongst spaceflight's most intriguing unsolved mysteries.

News.title: Hay fever sufferers warned about 5 trees as 'tree pollen season worse'
News.content:
Hay fever sufferers warned about 5 trees as 'tree pollen season worse'

An estimated 13 million people in the UK suffer from hay fever

View Image Scientists at the University of Worcester are predicting that high levels of pollen will continue for several weeks (Image: Getty)

People who suffer from hayfever have been warned to stay away from five types of tree - as the tree pollen season this year is particularly bad. An estimated 13 million people in the UK suffer from hay fever - the allergy to pollen.

‌



The Met Office says Tree pollen occurs first, typically from late March to mid-May, and affects around 25% of people. Most people are allergic to grass pollen (which actually has two peaks) and the season lasts from mid-May until July. Weed pollen can be released at any time but the season typically covers the end of June to September.

‌



Scientists at the University of Worcester are predicting that high levels will continue for several weeks - and the trees most likely to give it off are Birch, Oak, Cedar, Alder, and Hazel.

‌



Dr Beverley Adams-Groom, senior palynologist and pollen forecaster, said: “Trees that produce allergenic pollen in the UK tend to have alternating high and low years of severity – this year is a high year.”

High temperatures during last spring and early summer meant there was the right weather for pollen production, with a warm May being a major factor. Dr Adams-Groom added: “Mild, dry weather with breezes provides the best conditions for pollen release and that is what is forecast for the next week or so.”

‌



Day-to-day variations in weather can make a big difference to how much pollen is produced and how effectively it spreads. Rain can suppress pollen whereas warmth and sunshine tend to boost it - although on the very hottest days in summer levels can actually decrease.

Dry, mild, breezy days can bring the highest pollen counts. On the windiest days, pollen can be blown across a wide area meaning lower concentrations in any particular place.

The impact of climate change on pollen is likely to be mixed with considerable variations between species and in different parts of the UK.

Article continues below

Research by the University of Worcester found that the birch tree pollen season is getting more severe, while grass pollen continues to fluctuate around the long-term average. Most people are allergic to grass pollen (which actually has two peaks) and the season lasts from mid-May until July. Weed pollen can be released at any time but the season typically covers the end of June to September.

The Met Office said: “There are also loads of factors that change the start date of the pollen season. Low temperatures in winter will keep plants and trees dormant for longer into the new year. Essentially, the lower the temperature the less pollen is produced, but, this can change if soil and air temperatures in spring are higher than normal.

“Spring rainfall is also key, as a dry season reduces the amount of pollen production. Regardless of the weather, pollen is also dependent on how hardy different species are and how well they cope with a mixture of different types in one region.”

News.title: Morgan McSweeney failed to disclose No10 job after phone theft - see full 999 call
News.content:
Morgan McSweeney failed to disclose No10 job after phone theft - see full 999 call

The theft of the phone last year means key messages between Morgan McSweeney, the PM's former chief of staff, and his former mentor Peter Mandelson may have been lost

View 2 Images Keir Starmer's former chief of staff Morgan McSweeney reported his phone stolen (Image: Ian Vogler / Daily Mirror)

Morgan McSweeney failed to tell police he was Keir Starmer's chief of staff when his phone was stolen, a 999 call transcript reveals.

‌



In a highly unusual move, the Metropolitan Police released the log today in a furious riposte to reports they were "too busy" to investigate the theft of the device. The loss of the phone means messages between Mr McSweeney, the PM's former chief of staff, and his former mentor Peter Mandelson may have been lost.

‌



Keir Starmer has been compelled to publish a vast cache of emails, WhatsApps and reports about the decision to hand the plum post in Washington to the peer, despite his ties to Jeffrey Epstein. But it emerged at the weekend that Mr McSweeney's phone had been nicked in London in October, only weeks after Lord Mandelson was sacked.

‌



The call log shows Mr McSweeney said it was a Government phone but did not spell out the significance of his role in Downing Street or the sensitivity of the messages on his phone.

Peter Mandelson demanded £500,000 payout after sacking over Epstein ties READ MORE :

OPINION Mikey Smith Hidden secret in new photo of Andrew and Mandelson with Epstein that can't be unseen

View 2 Images Peter Mandelson was sacked as US ambassador last year over his ties to the paedophile Jeffrey Epstein (Image: AP)

‌



When the Mirror initially asked about the theft, Scotland Yard supplied a statement with the wrong address. It has now admitted that the call handler incorrectly recorded a street with a similar name in east London, rather than the real site of the crime in Pimlico.

A Met Police spokesperson said: “Having identified this error, the report will be amended and the assessment of whether there is available evidence revisited."

Releasing the full transcript of the call, the force said: "It has been assumed by many that the Met would have known details of the victim’s employment or the particular security risks associated with his device or material on it. This was not information provided to us and could not reasonably have shaped our decision making, as you will see below.

‌



"We would not typically release detailed information about an allegation of crime however given the profile of the incident, the public interest and in an effort to ensure any reporting is as accurate as possible, we have taken the decision to do so."

The theft of the phone raises doubts over whether key exchanges may have been lost. It comes after the first tranche of files revealed the PM was warned of the risks of appointing Lord Mandelson, with the appointment branded "weirdly rushed" by national security adviser Jonathan Powell.

Officials are trawling through tens of thousands of files for the next release, which is now expected to come after the Easter holiday. The next batch is believed to include communications between Lord Mandelson, and ministers and officials, which could prove embarrassing for senior Government figures.

‌



Mr McSweeney, who was a protégé of the Labour grandee, is credited with masterminding Mr Starmer's landslide election victory. He quit as the PM's chief of staff last month as the Mandelson scandal rocked the Government

Speaking at the time, Mr McSweeney said: "The decision to appoint Peter Mandelson was wrong. He has damaged our party, our country and trust in politics itself. When asked, I advised the Prime Minister to make that appointment and I take full responsibility for that advice.

"In public life responsibility must be owned when it matters most, not just when it is most convenient. In the circumstances, the only honourable course is to step aside."

‌



Read the transcript of the call in full

Call handler: Police, what's your emergency?

Caller: Oh, hello, someone just robbed my phone.

Call handler: Did they actually take it from you just now?

‌



Caller: Yeah

Call handler: How did they get away?

Caller: So he's on a bike. He's come onto the pavement to grab my phone and cycled off on a bike.

‌



Call handler: And where did this happen?

Caller: It happened in Belgrave Street* in Westminster.

*We now know that the incident took place in Belgrave Road, Westminster. The call handler inputs Belgrave Street and it provides a matching road name in Tower Hamlets, which is what is recorded in error. There are further references to locations near to Belgrave Street in Tower Hamlets later in the call, which compounds the issue.

‌



Call handler: And whose phone are you using now?

Caller: I've got two phones. I'm using my personal one. That was my work one.

Call handler: Can I take the phone number for this phone you're calling on?

‌



Caller: Yeah, 07XXXXXXXXX.

Call handler: Thank you. And you said Belgrave Street, yeah?

Caller: Yeah, just kind of going back to the location.

‌



Call handler: Don't put yourself at any risk. It's not worth it over a phone. I appreciate it’s frustrating.

Call handler: And which way did they go towards, this suspect on a bike?

Caller: He went. He travels north. I saw him for a few blocks.

‌



Call handler: So where were you when you last saw him? Have you got any idea?

Caller: Yeah, so.

Call handler: Did you get up to Stepney?

‌



Caller: Let me tell you where I got to. I'm just going back to where I can.

Caller: So he turned right. Sorry, he turned left. There's a park on top of the road and he turned left there.

Call handler: Stepney Green Park, ok.

‌



Caller: Yeah. He turned left there.

Call handler: Can you remember anything about his appearance?

Caller: Yeah, he was young. He was a black guy. He was on a bike.

‌



Call handler: About how young?

Call handler: Just a guess.

Caller: Teens. Late teens.

‌



Call handler: Was he skinny, tall, any idea?

Caller: Yeah. He was slim. He was about average height.

Call handler: Was it an e bike or pedal bike?

‌



Caller: Pedal bike.

Call handler: Have you got a tracker on the phone at all?

Caller: I do. It’s a government phone.

‌



Call handler: And it's your work phone. What kind of phone is it?

Caller: It’s an iPhone.

Call handler: Do you know what model?

‌



Caller: I don't.

[PAUSE]

Call handler: Right, just bear with me a second.

‌



Call handler: We would normally deploy to see you but at the moment, we are having extreme demand on police officers. So, I don't know if you would prefer to make your way home and make a crime report over the phone or online tomorrow. I mean, I can complete one with you now. I can pass this down, you can wait, but I honestly do not know how long you'll be waiting,

Caller: If I could complete it now that would be good.

Call handler: Ok.

‌



Call handler: What's your name, please?

Caller: My name is XXXXXXXXXX.

Call handler: XXXXXXXXXX? (repeats name back)

‌



Caller: Yeah.

Call handler: And your date of birth, please?

Caller: It's XXXXXX

‌



Call handler: Is XXXXXXXXX (surname) all one word?

Caller: Yeah, (spells surname).

Call handler: And what's your home address?

‌



Caller: (Provides non-London address)

Call handler: So you live in XXXXXX?

Caller: Yeah.

‌



Call handler: Are you staying anywhere while you're in London?

Caller: Yeah.

Call handler: Sorry, it just takes a little bit longer to deal with an address outside of the Met. I do apologise.

‌



Caller: It’s ok.

Call handler: And may I take an email for you please (name)?

Caller: Yeah, it's XXXXXXXXXXX@XXXXXXXXXX.com (personal email address)

‌



Call handler: You'll get a copy of the preliminary crime report through to that email.

Call handler: How would you like to be contacted by an investigating officer? By email or phone?

Caller: Phone, please. Or either, I’m not fussed.

‌



Call handler: Have you got any finance apps on the phone?

Caller: No.

Call handler: You'll need to change any passwords for any logins you do have on the phone.

‌



Caller: Yeah, okay.

Call handler: You're not vulnerable in any way. Are you?

Caller: No I’m not.

‌



Call handler: Do you believe there was any CCTV near where the incident happened?

Caller: Might be. [Inaudible] away from location.

Call handler: Don't worry. Don't return. No, I'll just put at the moment unknown. And obviously, if we find out more, we find out more.

‌



Call handler: Are you willing to make a statement to support the investigation?

Caller: Definitely.

Call handler: So what time did he actually snatch the phone?

‌



Caller: About two minutes before I rung you and I chased, and then I rang my office to get the phone tracked and then I rang you.

Call handler: Okay, cool. It would have been about 25 past that you were robbed.

Caller: A little before, about 23 minutes past, I think.

‌



Call handler: 23? Little bit before? Okay.

[PAUSE]

Call handler: Just bear with me, I’m just trying to get this system to accept the address. Sorry about this. I won't keep you much longer.

‌



Call handler: If you do get any tracking updates, what you do is you give us call back if the phone is stationary.

Caller: Yeah.

Call handler: And we can review attending then. We can't guarantee attending a moving phone at all, but if it's been stationary for a few…

‌



Call handler: It’s not accepting your address.

Caller: I can give you my London address?

Call handler: It’s alright. I've nearly got this to work.

‌



Caller: Okay

Call handler: How long you staying in London?

Caller: So I come to London every week. I work in London.

‌



Call handler: Oh, I see. Okay, that makes sense.

Caller: So I'll be here till Thursday.

Call handler: Okay.

‌



[PAUSE]

Call handler: As I was trying to say, I've got this sorted now, so I'll be texting you a crime reference number in the next few moments. Along with the crime reference number will be a CHS reference number. If you need to give us a call back, you can call back giving that reference number from any device, and then we'll be able to link it straight away to your crime report and review deploying. We will need to know a bit more details about the phone itself, so when you're contacted by the investigating officer, or if you do get tracking details, you can call us back with the IMEI number, and the type of phone that it is that would be super helpful.

Caller: All right, thank you.

‌



Call handler: All right, I’m just about to text you through the crime reference number now.

Caller: Thank you so much. You’ve been really helpful.

Call handler: No worries. All right, (name). You take care now, okay? Bye.

Article continues below

Caller: Bye bye.

News.title: BBC Morning Live doctor busts 3 dental home remedies that ‘don’t work’
News.content:
Toothache is one of the most common oral health problems people in the UK are facing. It can become a debilitating issue as people wait or delay getting professional help, but one doctor has warned some home ‘remedies’ may be doing more harm than good when trying to treat the issue at home.

Doctor Xand highlighted some home remedies that can actually help subside the pain and explained why toothache can affect people so differently to every other type of pain in the body. He said on BBC’s Morning Live: “Your teeth have many different sorts of pain receptors in them.”

He explained that when other parts of the body are injured, it can swell and you may be able to avoid using it in order to recover. However, your teeth cannot swell and are always being used in vital functions like eating, this makes toothache much more difficult and painful to deal with.

Toothache can be debilitating and relentless, leaving people willing to try almost anything to fix it while they await dental appointments and more permanent fixes. However, the doctor warned against following some “dangerous advice online”.

Image: GETTY) GETTY)

Dr Xand advised people not to try the chewing cloves of garlic trick: “It’s a very intense experience. It'll make your mouth burn. It'll make your eyes water. It might transiently take your mind.

“To be honest, it's going to make everything worse. Garlic is an irritant. You make the pain worse, you can make the inflammation worse. Do not go down that route.

“There's lots of other dangerous advice online. You'll see people telling you to gargle with diluted bleach, things like that. Do not do any of this.”

Rubbing aspirin on the gums has a similar issue because it’s an acidic irritant which can make the pain and inflammation worse. Dr Xand recommended doing a salt water rinse instead, but warned it’s “not going to fix the problem” and people with sensitive teeth may need to watch the temperature of the water they are using.

Finally, swishing whiskey around your aching teeth is an age old home remedy that isn’t doing much. The doctor added: “Maybe it's a bit antiseptic but whiskey burns. It's very inflammatory. It's a waste of whiskey. Definitely don't swallow it because you don't want to be drunk and have a toothache. Then you can't get to the dentist.”

Dr Xand highlighted some remedies which can actually treat toothache: “You want is oral painkillers that are paracetamol and ibuprofen. You can take the two together very safely. Most people can do that but read the instructions and take the biggest dose. That will take the edge off the pain at two in the morning.”

Image: GETTY) GETTY)

He also recommended using a cold compress, which can lower the inflammation and reduce blood flow to “take the edge off” and he warned heat may make things worse. It’s best to wrap the cold compress or frozen bag of peas in a tea towel to avoid getting cold burns on your face.

Finally, gently brushing your teeth may help dislodge any stuck pieces of food that may be making the pain worse. Ultimately Dr Xand urged: “The most important thing to say is go to a dentist. One of the things that makes toothache much worse is anxiety. It's a very anxiety-dependent pain.

“When you get toothache, the first thing you stop worrying about is, will I be able to see a dentist. If you possibly can, register with an NHS dentist early. Get involved and start looking after your teeth.”

News.title: Man walking in the woods spots apex predator that 'shouldn't be here'
News.content:
Man walking in the woods spots apex predator that 'shouldn't be here'

A wildlife expert tracked down a non-native apex predator that has been living wild in a woodland area in the UK for around three years. He believes she may have been brought to the country illegally

View Image An apex predator was spotted living in the woods in the UK (stock image) (Image: Marcin Kanka via Getty Images)

A man tracked down "unexpected" apex predator livng here in the UK, leaving him convinced she's been brought to the country illegally. It's can be quite the shock to stumble across animals that aren't typically found on our land - especially apex predators.

‌



They play a crucial role in various ecosystems; they sit at the top of the food chain with no natural enemies or predators. Examples of apex predators includes creatures like orcas, great white sharks, tigers, brown bears, komodo dragons, lions and more.

‌



While the UK isn't home to standard apex predators, we do have animal species that dominate their respective habitats, such as golden eagles and red foxes. So it was quite a shock when a man visited the West Midlands and discovered an apex predator "living in the wild in Wolverhampton".

‌



James Cook, who shares content online as Wildlife with Cookie, has amassed over 165,000 followers on Instagram, where he focuses on wildlife and nature.

In a recent collection of videos and photos, he chronicled his quest to track down "an apex predator that should NOT be in the UK," suspecting she may have been "illegally" brought into the country.

The predator in question? An eagle owl, one of the largest species of owl, which is not native to the UK. They're naturally found in other parts of Europe as well as Asia, while some species like the spotted eagle owl live in southern Africa.

‌



People had been flocking to capture images of the owl following sightings at Wightwick Manor, a beloved Victorian manor house and gardens that draws tourists to Wolverhampton. Many had contacted James seeking confirmation of the species.

Equipped with "camera batteries, thermal binoculars, action camera", James successfully located "the beast", documenting his adventure on the popular social media platform. He revealed Wightwick Manor staff told him the owl had been spotted in the area on and off for around three years.

Content cannot be displayed without consent

‌



Alongside a collection of photos, he explained how he found the "apex predator in the UK" and continued: "It's not every day you get to see an Eagle Owl in the wild, let alone living wild and free here in the UK! But recently, I was fortunate enough to track one down in Wolverhampton of all places?!"

He praised thermal technology as a "game changer when locating animals" since "even owls can't hide".

James said: "Of all the information I managed to gather of this gorgeous looking beast, it appears that it's female, has no ID ring on her leg and has been living wild in the Wolverhampton area for around 3 years.

‌



"I noticed a half eaten rat on the floor underneath her so I can only assume she's thriving!"

But she "shouldn't be here".

He added: "The Verreauxs Eagle Owl would NOT be able to travel to the UK on its own accord and therefore this is most likely a pet that has been released or has escaped captivity - due to the lack of an ID leg ring, I'm led to believe she has potentially been illegally kept and then illegally released.

‌



"What's for certain though, is she's absolutely gorgeous and a rather unexpected sight here in the UK."

In the Instagram comments, fellow users were eager to weigh in. One said: "She's sooo beautiful but I can't help but feel sad for her living alone in the UK. I also wouldn't give out the location as people will end up disturbing her or worse trying to harm her."

Another shared similar concern: "Hope if she has to be then she's captured and not killed, she has been there for a while surviving on her own."

Article continues below

A third said: "It's great that it managed to survive the totally different climate! Still, poor guy or girl doesn't have a mate, very sad."

Someone else commented: "That's crazy Cookie, what a great find though."

James reassured followers: "It's on National Trust land, it's safe."

News.title: BREAKING: Sweden avalanche leaves multiple people trapped as 999 crews swarm scene
News.content:
Multiple people have been left trapped in an avalanche at a popular ski resort, sparking a frantic search and rescue operation.

The avalanche hit Sweden's Kiruna municipality just southeast of Riksgränsen, with initial reports suggesting ten people had been swept away before disappearing underneath the snow. At least seven fire engines and two ambulances have been scrambled to the scene.

Officers received an alert at 1:45pm local time today, but it took rescuers 45 minutes to reach the mountain, the service's command operator said. According to local police, five people were dug out of the snow by others on the mountain before rescuers arrived.

Image: Getty Images/Johner RF) Getty Images/Johner RF)

All five people have been taken to hospital for check ups, while rescue teams remain at the scene to try and locate anyone else who may be trapped. It is feared up to ten people are still unaccounted for, line operator Engla Hansson told Aftonbladet.

Officers have called Norway to assist with extra personnel and a rescue helicopter.

This is a breaking news story.

News.title: 'Hero' grandad, 92, dies before claiming litter-picking award
News.content:
EXCLUSIVE: 'Hero' grandad, 92, dies before claiming litter-picking award

So far, over 370,000 bags have been pledged to be filled for the 11th Great British Spring Clean organised by Keep Britain Tidy and proudly supported by the Mirror

View 4 Images Retiree Derek Cole, 92, who litter-picked his street twice a week, won the Litter Legend category after being nominated by his proud daughter, who described him as a "hero”, "legend" and an "inspiration".

Armed with bin bags, litter pickers and endless enthusiasm, these spring clean heroes have helped to hold back the tide of trash engulfing Britain. So far, over 370,000 bags have been pledged to be filled for the 11th Great British Spring Clean organised by Keep Britain Tidy and proudly supported by the Daily Mirror. And there’s still time to take part and help protect our wildlife, beaches and green spaces, between now and March 29.

‌



Volunteers joining the two weeks of litter-picking action include Callum MacLean, 34, a communications manager from National Highways. He was a runner-up at the Keep Britain Tidy awards for his humorous videos highlighting the impact of rubbish dumped on the roads and showing some of the most unusual finds. Discarded tyres, fancy dress costumes, fairground rides, Scrabble pieces and even a bag of cash, have been dumped in lay-bys and on roundabouts, as well as countless items of food packaging littering the verges.

‌



Owl can't fly and desperate baby hedgehog tries to escape RSPCA issues heartbreaking plea READ MORE :

Such mindless dumping has a huge impact, with an estimated three million animals killed each year on the side of roads - drawn to sticky cans, packets and wrappers, containing food and drink residue. Callum said: “Litter threatens wildlife living alongside our roads. It also puts road users and those who have to pick it up at risk. People think throwing an apple core out of the window is fine, because it’s biodegradable, but it encourages wildlife to come closer to roads and is often fatal.”

‌



View 4 Images Callum, a highways officer, has been highlighting the rubbish on the M6 motorway (Image: Ian Vogler / Daily Mirror)

Asking drivers to take rubbish home or to the nearest motorway services, he added: “You wouldn’t necessarily lean out your bedroom window and throw whatever’s out there onto the street. “That’s effectively what’s happening on our roads. Perhaps it is a case of out of sight, out of mind, but the impact can be devastating for British wildlife.”

A fifth (22%) of adults in the UK have admitted to littering on or near the roadside in the last 12 months, according to a National Highways study, while nearly two-thirds ( 62%) of UK licence holders say they do consider that litter can injure or kill animals living near the roadside .

‌



Schoolgirl Abigail Comben, 11, has collected more than 5,000 litres of litter near her home at Blakeney Woods, Sunderland, and will turn out again this year. Her dedication which has seen her attend 42 organised community tidy ups over the last three years - equating to 84 hours of voluntary work - saw her win the Young Litter Legend award, with judges calling her passion and enthusiasm “infectious.”

View 4 Images A fifth (22%) of adults in the UK have admitted to littering on or near the roadside in the last 12 months, according to a National Highways study (Image: Ian Vogler / Daily Mirror)

Last Easter she made a short animated film with friends at the youth club about looking after the environment - showing young people collecting litter and returning nearby Blakeney Woods back to how it should be. Retiree Derek Cole, 92, who litter-picked his street twice a week, won the Litter Legend category after being nominated by his proud daughter, who described him as a "hero”, "legend" and an "inspiration".

‌



Despite having a couple of operations over the last few years, leaving him walking with a stick or frame, he was well known locally for his regular solo litter-picks - especially clearing drains of rubbish during heavy rain. Last year, he took part in the Great British Spring Clean armed with his bucket and litter-picker, scooping up the cigarette butts, among other things dropped by visitors to local pubs and restaurants.

Sadly, Derek, of Cheltenham, Gloucestershire, died in December, before learning he’d won the award. His proud family received it on his behalf at an event in the House of Commons to mark the start of this year’s Great British Spring Clean earlier this month.

View 4 Images Keep Britain Tidy logo (Image: Keep Britain Tidy)

Article continues below

Daughter Susan Cole said: “Dad was well known locally as he was very community minded. Litter picking was just one of the many things he did to help out. He would have been thrilled to receive the award. His neighbours have told me they're now carrying on what he started. We are all so honoured that he was chosen to receive the award and proud that he has left a litter picking legacy in his community.”

HOW TO TAKE PART

Boxing.title: Usyk invited me and Anthony Joshua to Kyiv… here's what he told me about loss, AJ's return to boxing and war: CHARLOTTE DALY
Boxing.content:
Kyiv, March 21, 2026. A date I will never forget. Amid the echoes of sirens and hollowed-out streets, two heavyweight champions and rivals stand side by side in a compelling image of unity.

Oleksandr Usyk, the pride of Ukraine and former undisputed heavyweight champion, and Anthony Joshua, Britain’s two-time world champion, have come together not for a fight, but for something perhaps even more powerful: a shared purpose, a journey into the heart of a nation, and a bond forged through respect, grief and ambition.

We are here to see Usyk. No one knew that Joshua would be coming too. Every detail of the Brit's journey has been carefully planned.
